clinical_history: The ECG shows which ONE of the following diagnoses?
diagnosis: c)  The ECG shows typical atrial flutter waves. The ventricular response is not highly irregular as in atrial fibrillation, but is also not strictly regular.  The rate here has a definite pattern; the conduction pattern is not consistently 3:1 or 4:1, but rather more variable.  Parkinsonian tremor may simulate atrial flutter or fibrillation. In such cases the underlying rhythm is usually recognizable upon close inspection.
